Question: Should patients with RA on DMARDs who are in low disease activity gradually taper off DMARDs, abruptly withdraw DMARDs, or continue DMARDS at the same doses?
P: Adults with rheumatoid arthritis (RA) who are on stable conventional synthetic or biologic disease-modifying antirheumatic drugs (DMARDs) and have achieved sustained low disease activity (e.g., DAS28 <3.2 for ≥6 months)
I: Gradual tapering of DMARDs (e.g., dose reduction or extended dosing intervals)
C: Abrupt withdrawal of DMARDs or continuation of DMARDs at the same doses

Abrupt withdrawal of DMARDs or continuat: Risk of disease flare (defined as increase in DAS28 ≥1.2 or meeting ACR/EULAR Boolean criteria for high disease activity), radiographic progression, functional disability (HAQ-DI), and sustained remission
